extra.reasoning: The property globally(opted_out implies not sent) holds on all reachable executions. Proof by invariant preservation. The invariant "opted_out implies not sent" holds in the initial state (opted_out=false, vacuously true). Each transition preserves it: t1 (optout) sets opted_out:=true and simultaneously sent:=false, so the consequent is satisfied. t2 (send_attempt) is the ONLY transition that sets sent:=true, but its guard is "not opted_out", so after t2 fires opted_out is false and the implication is vacuously true. t3 (send_attempt when opted_out) sets sent:=false. t4 (tick) always sets sent:=false. Crucially, no transition ever assigns opted_out:=false, so once opted_out becomes true it remains true, and the only transition that could set sent=true (t2) is permanently disabled (guard requires not opted_out). Therefore every reachable state with opted_out=true has sent=false. The invariant is inductive and holds for executions of any length, including up to 6 events. No counterexample exists.
